message:
fix(channel): poison-safe drops in broadcast and oneshot, add RecvFuture waker cleanup

Broadcast: Sender::drop and Receiver::drop now use `let Ok(mut inner) =
self.channel.inner.lock() else { return }` instead of `.expect()`. When
the std::sync::Mutex is poisoned (another thread panicked while holding
the lock), the previous code would panic in drop, causing a double-panic
abort during unwinding. The new code silently bails — the channel is
already in a broken state, and waking through a poisoned lock would be
unsound. Adds two regression tests verifying that sender and receiver
drops on a poisoned mutex do not panic.

Oneshot: Apply the same poison-safe bail pattern to Sender::drop,
SendPermit::drop, and Receiver::drop. Additionally implements
RecvFuture::Drop to unconditionally clear the registered waker when the
future is dropped while Pending (e.g., as a select/race loser). Without
this, a stale waker would retain executor state for a future that no
longer exists. Since oneshot has at most one receiver, unconditionally
clearing is always correct. Updates the existing
permit_send_receiver_dropped_clears_waker test to assert
waker.is_none() after RecvFuture drop (previously it asserted is_some()).
Adds dedicated tests for: recv_future_drop_clears_stale_waker (verifies
channel still works after drop), and poison-safety tests for sender,
permit, and receiver drops.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
